name: rule id namespacing: when a rule needs renaming, the old id is marked deprecated:true with replaced_by:[<new-id>] (E-10 pathway), not silently renamed
command:
mkdir -p tree/google && printf 'source:
  id: g
  authoritative_publisher: G
  authoritative_url: "https://g.com"
  registry_link: STANDARDS-URLS.yaml
  fetched_at: "2026-05-04T00:00:00Z"
  content_hash: "sha256:S"
  fetch_frequency: daily
  fragility_tier: low
  license_note: MIT
rules:
  - id: "g-old-001"
    name: x
    description: x
    detector: x
    type: problem
    fixable: null
    has_suggestions: false
    deprecated: true
    replaced_by: ["g-new-001"]
    docs_url: "https://x.com"
    requires_type_checking: false
    recommended: false
    options_schema: {}
    messages:
      default: x
    severity: P1
    version: 1.0.0
    semver: 1.0.0
    rule_state: disabled
    provenance:
      - source: x
        class: published-standard
        section_id: x
        url: "https://x.com"
        fetched_at: "2026-05-04T00:00:00Z"
        content_hash: "sha256:S"
  - id: "g-new-001"
    name: x
    description: x
    detector: x
    type: problem
    fixable: null
    has_suggestions: false
    deprecated: false
    replaced_by: []
    docs_url: "https://x.com"
    requires_type_checking: false
    recommended: true
    options_schema: {}
    messages:
      default: x
    severity: P1
    version: 1.0.0
    semver: 1.0.0
    rule_state: warn-only
    provenance:
      - source: x
        class: published-standard
        section_id: x
        url: "https://x.com"
        fetched_at: "2026-05-04T00:00:00Z"
        content_hash: "sha256:S"
recommended_set: ["g-new-001"]
all_set: ["g-old-001", "g-new-001"]
' > tree/google/x.yaml && bash "$CLAUDE_PLUGIN_ROOT/generated-code-quality-standards/validate-all.sh" --root "$PWD/tree" --format json --include-rules 2>out.json && grep -E '"id":"g-old-001","deprecated":true' out.json 1>&2 && grep -E '"replaced_by":\["g-new-001"\]' out.json 1>&2 && echo ok 1>&2
